irrigation_systems.name: Drip Irrigation
advantages: Saves 40–60% water compared to flood irrigation
irrigation_systems.subsidy: 50–80% subsidy available under PMKSY Har Khet Ko Pani and state schemes
irrigation_systems.maintenance: Flush lateral pipes weekly. Check emitters for clogging. Maintain filter regularly.

irrigation_systems.name: Sprinkler Irrigation
advantages: Good for sandy soils with low water retention
irrigation_systems.maintenance: Clean nozzles. Check pressure. Operate at optimum pressure (1.5–3 kg/cm2).

irrigation_systems.name: Flood/Furrow Irrigation
irrigation_systems.improvement: Convert to drip/sprinkler for water savings.

irrigation_scheduling.principle: Irrigate based on soil moisture status and crop water requirement, not fixed calendar schedules

methods.description: Take soil sample at 15 cm depth. Squeeze in hand. If moist ball forms – OK; if dry crumbles – irrigate; if water squeezes out – excess.
Install at 15 and 30 cm depth. Irrigate when reading crosses 30–40 centibars (crop-specific).
Irrigate when cumulative pan evaporation (CPE) reaches crop-specific threshold. IW:CPE ratio of 1.0 for most field crops.

water_conservation.description: Cover soil surface with organic (straw, dry leaves) or plastic mulch
water_conservation.benefit: Reduces evaporation by 30–40%. Controls weeds. Improves soil temperature.

water_conservation.description: Ploughing and planting along contour lines on slopes
water_conservation.benefit: Reduces runoff by 50%. Increases water infiltration. Prevents soil erosion.

water_conservation.description: Excavate farm pond to harvest rainwater for supplemental irrigation
water_conservation.benefit: Provides critical irrigation during dry spells. Useful for horticulture crops.
water_conservation.subsidy: Government provides 50% subsidy for farm pond construction under PMKSY

water_conservation.description: Plant crops on raised beds with furrows for irrigation
water_conservation.benefit: Saves 20–25% water. Better drainage. Improved aeration. Easy intercultural operations.

water_conservation.description: Young seedlings transplanted single, wider spacing, intermittent irrigation (AWD)
water_conservation.benefit: Saves 30–50% water. Increases yield by 20–30%. Reduces methane emissions.

EC_tolerance.sensitive: Rice, Beans, Onion, Carrot – EC < 1.5 dS/m
EC_tolerance.tolerant: Barley, Sugarbeet, Date Palm – EC > 4.0 dS/m

water_quality.notes: High-sodium water (SAR > 10) degrades soil structure. Use gypsum to reclaim sodic soils.
